<!-- Topic 5: Fill In the Missing Code #1 -->
<!-- Total slides: 3 -->

# Fill In the Missing Code #1

---

## Exercise: scalar new/delete with error checking {.smaller}

+ The program allocates a single `double` on the heap, reads a value into it, and reports the result.
+ Fill in the missing block to make it compile and run correctly.

::: notes
Total slides: 3
Students need to write: the new allocation with nothrow, the nullptr check, and the delete + nullptr assignment. About 4 lines in a contiguous block.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## The code — fill in lines 10–13 {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void printDouble(const double* p) {
    cout << "Value: " << *p << "\n";
}

int main() {
    double* temp;

    // === YOUR CODE HERE (lines 10–13) ===
    // Allocate a double on the heap using nothrow.
    // If allocation failed, print an error and return 1.
    // Otherwise, read a value from the user into *temp.
    // =====================================

    printDouble(temp);

    delete temp;
    temp = nullptr;
    return 0;
}
```

<!-- Slide 2 -->

---

## Solution {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void printDouble(const double* p) {
    cout << "Value: " << *p << "\n";
}

int main() {
    double* temp;

    temp = new (nothrow) double;
    if (temp == nullptr) {
        cerr << "Allocation failed\n";
        return 1;
    }
    cin >> *temp;

    printDouble(temp);

    delete temp;
    temp = nullptr;
    return 0;
}
```

<!-- Slide 3 -->
